market_overview.headline: Broad market remains in full bear EMA structure with geopolitical tensions weighing on sentiment.
market_overview.detail: Only 41% of stocks in Full Bull configuration while 36% are Full Bear, with SPY, QQQ, and BTC all showing Full Bear signals despite potential bullish crossover setups developing.

momentum_themes.headline: Tech Hardware Leading Momentum
momentum_themes.detail: Electronic technology names dominate momentum leaders with optical components and semiconductors showing explosive moves. Storage and networking infrastructure benefiting from AI buildout acceleration.

sector_analysis.headline: Energy Dominates, Tech Services Crater

strongest.note: Perfect bullish score as Iran tensions boost oil complex across the board.
Infrastructure and services benefiting from energy sector strength and defense spending.
Mining and materials holding up well in inflationary geopolitical environment.

weakest.note: Software and cloud services under severe pressure with only 12.5% bullish.
Auto and housing-related stocks completely bearish as rates and war fears bite.
Banks struggling despite potential rate benefits as credit concerns mount.

sector_analysis.detail: Clear flight to defensive energy and materials while growth tech gets hammered. Geopolitical premium favoring old economy over new economy assets.

items.text: Major indices all in Full Bear EMA structure with potential for deeper correction
Technology services sector showing 87.5% bearish readings — avoid unless reversal confirmed
Iran war escalation driving oil prices to records — supply disruption risk mounting
Consumer durables at 100% bearish — housing and auto sectors in deep trouble

news_drivers.headline: What Moved Markets
news_drivers.summary: Iran war escalation dominated headlines with oil hitting record premiums, Strait of Hormuz blockade talks, and Trump vowing continued attacks. SpaceX IPO news provided brief distraction but geopolitical fears kept markets defensive despite potential index EMA crossovers developing.

items.text: Iran war intensification sent oil prices to record premiums as supply fears escalated
40 countries discussing Strait of Hormuz reopening while Iran threatens further blockades
Defense stocks initially surged but gave back gains as war premium already priced in
SpaceX IPO speculation provided brief tech boost but couldn't overcome broader risk-off sentiment
Markets showing cautious optimism with low volume despite headline risks
